title: taraxacum rupicaprae
intro: Taraxacum rupicaprae, also known as Alpine Hawk's Beard or Alpine Dandelion, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and jagged leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which make it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and urinary disorders. The health benefits of Taraxacum rupicaprae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum rupicaprae include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the plant's roots and leaves in hot water.
